zh.Hodnet, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾霍德特
zh.De La Hull, Nash Burford, Shropshire, En: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾納什
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggard於1275年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾霍德特。她於1292年與Sir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hull在英國史洛普爾霍德特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1300年在她的家鄉享年25歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggard于1275年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾霍德特。她于1292年与Sir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hull在英国史洛普爾霍德特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1300年在她的家乡享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Giffard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Continental Germanic personal name Gifard. (ii) nickname, perhaps sometimes from Old French giffard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Jefford
meanings: English:  see  Giffard  .

variants.name: Guiver
meanings: English: (i) nickname, apparently from Middle English giver ‘giver, donor, bestower’. (ii) variant of Giffard with voicing of /f/ to /v/ and loss of final -d.

variants.name: Gafford
meanings: English (southeastern): assimilated form of Gatford, a name of various origins. In Sussex and Surrey it is perhaps a variant of the habitational name Catford from a place so called in Somerset. Gatford is also from the habitational names Gateford (in Worksop, Nottinghamshire) or Gateforth (near Selby, Yorkshire), both formed with Old English gāt ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’, influenced by Old Norse geit ‘goat’.

Probably also an altered form of German Gaffert: habitational name from Gaffert near Köslin, Brandenburg, or from a personal name formed with Middle High German gate ‘fellow, companion’. The surname Gaffert is very rare in Germany.

English: perhaps an altered form of Gatewood , an occupational name for a ‘goatherd’ (Middle English gate-werde, Old English gātweard) or for a ‘gate keeper’ (Middle English gateward, Old English ʒeatweard, dialect gātward).

variants.name: Gifford
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jefferd
meanings: English:  see  Giffard  .

nameMeanings.name: Baggard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of Hawkyard, a habitational name from Hawk Yard in Saddleworth (Yorkshire).

English: nickname from Middle English, Old French hagard ‘wild, untamed’. This word was adopted into Middle English as a technical term in falconry to denote a hawk that had been captured and trained when already fully grown, rather than being reared in captivity; the surname may have developed as a metonymic occupational name for a falconer.
English: possibly also a variant of Agar with prosthetic H- and excrescent -d.

variants.name: Agar
meanings: from a Middle English personal name, either Egar (see Edgar ) or Algar (see Alger ).
variant of Hager , with loss of initial H-.
English:

variants.name: Haggar
meanings: English: variant of   Haggard   or   Agar  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Madam
meanings: Hebrew: The Scripture name, in Hebrew, Adham, signifying 'man;' literally earthy, red earth; Rudolph, however, says the name in Ethiopic means ' to be fair, beautiful.' Hence the nurse-name Addy, little Adam.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

en.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggard was born in 1275, in Hodnet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hull in 1292, in Hodnet, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1300, in her hometown, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggard est née en 1275, à Hodnet, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hull en 1292, à Hodnet, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1300, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggard wurde 1275 in Hodnet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1292 in Hodnet, Shropshire, England Sir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hull geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1300 im Alter von 25 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggard è nata il nel 1275 nel Hodnet, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Sir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hull nel 1292, nel Hodnet, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1300, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 25 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggardは1275年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアホードネットで生まれた。1292年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアホードネットでSir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hullと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1300年に故郷で25歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Madam Alice Giffard Baggard(은)는 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 호드넷에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1292년 영국 슈럽실르 호드넷에서 Sir. Richard White Mere Hill de  la Hull(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1300년 그녀의 고향에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.
